item.title: Bubble Risks Are Rising as AI Borrowing Balloons
item.description: Some debt of AI players is better than others.

item.title: Blackstone-Led Creditors Take Over Ailing Software Firm Medallia
item.description: Medallia Inc. is being taken over by a group of creditors led by Blackstone Inc. after its owner Thoma Bravo said it wasn’t going to inject fresh cash into the ailing software-as-a-service company.

item.title: UK Inflation Set to Peak Below BOE’s Best-Case Scenario
item.description: UK inflation is on track to peak at levels well below even the Bank of England’s most optimistic scenario after the prospect of an end to the war in Iran triggered dramatic falls on global energy markets.

item.title: US Physical Crude Prices Ease as Middle Eastern Barrels Return
item.description: The wartime risk premium that propelled physical US crude prices higher is rapidly evaporating as concerns over Middle Eastern supplies ease and with Persian Gulf barrels set to return to the market.

item.title: Higher Bond Yields Are Here to Stay in a Post-War World
item.description: Higher government spending is still worrying investors.

item.title: Manipal Hospitals Is Said to Plan $1 Billion India IPO in July
item.description: Manipal Health Enterprises Pvt., which runs the Manipal Hospitals chain, is likely to launch its initial public offering as early as next month, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Brexit Bears Some Blame for EU’s Capital Market Inertia, EIB Chief Says
item.description: The UK’s exit from the European Union is partly responsible for the bloc’s failure to integrate its capital markets, the European Investment Bank president said.

item.title: Starmer Hot Mic Moment Suggests Breakthrough in India Trade Row
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer raised expectations that Britain has resolved a trade impasse with India, when he and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi were caught on a microphone at the G7 summit in France saying some form of agreement had been reached.

item.title: Third Iran-Linked Crude Carrier Crosses US Blockade Toward Asia
item.description: A third fully-loaded crude tanker has left the Iranian port of Chabahar and crossed the US blockade line heading toward Asia, just days before the planned signing of a ceasefire that will give Tehran the right to sell oil immediately.

item.title: Rupee Hits Six-Week High as India’s Inflow Measures Draw Demand
item.description: India’s rupee rallied to the strongest level in six weeks, fueling bets the currency can extend gains helped by the authorities’ steps this month to boost foreign-currency inflows.

item.title: Aviation Debt Boom Rolls On as Mideast Tensions Ease Further
item.description: Athens International Airport SA and TAP Air Portugal are adding to a record start to the year for borrowing by the aviation sector globally, as the industry looks to navigate the impact of the Middle East war.

item.title: What Comes After AI Replaces Jobs?
item.description: Guaranteed income. AI dividends. ‘Basic compute.’ Here are the proposed solutions if the technology wipes out millions of jobs.

item.title: Wall Street Wants to Cryptofy Your Stocks
item.description: Putting stocks and other assets on the blockchain can make trading faster, smoother and available 24/7, say the technology’s boosters.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Set to Brave Inflation Minefield With Interest Rate Cut
item.description: Brazil’s central bank will likely cut its key interest rate by a quarter-point on Wednesday, with an Iran peace deal easing some pressure as policymakers navigate a series of inflation risks.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Gimber Sees Value in European Stocks After Oil Shock
item.description: European equities offer value opportunities following the recent decline in oil prices, particularly consumer-facing stocks and energy-sensitive cyclicals, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Hugh Gimber.

item.title: Japan’s Humble 10-Yen Coin Now Worth 10.4 Yen On AI Copper Rally
item.description: Japan’s 10-yen coin, predominantly composed of copper, is now worth more in metal than its face value as AI-fueled demand boosts prices for the highly conductive material.

item.title: Singapore’s Sea Tests AI Chatbot Migoo in Quiet US Foray
item.description: Singapore internet leader Sea Ltd. is rolling out a generative-AI chatbot in regions including the US, marking its maiden foray into the highly competitive American consumer market and accelerating its artificial intelligence push.

item.title: UAE Moves to Cut Dependency on Strait of Hormuz to ‘Zero’
item.description: As the world awaits the full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz following the signing of an interim peace deal between Iran and the US, the United Arab Emirates is working on a highly ambitious plan to try to end its dependence on the critical chokepoint.

item.title: UK Borrowing Costs Hit Two-Month Low as Rate Hike Chances Slip
item.description: Gilts extended their wining streak to five days after UK inflation held steady in May, easing pressure on the Bank of England to raise interest rates.

item.title: SpaceX Heads Toward Fourth Day of Gains Since Record IPO
item.description: SpaceX shares are poised for a fourth straight day of gains, reinforcing the company’s place among the world’s largest after it surpassed Amazon.com Inc. by market value.

item.title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth to Quicken in Second Half of This Year
item.description: Euro-area pay growth is set to accelerate in the second half of this year, while remaining far below previous peaks, the European Central Bank said as it gauges inflation risks from the Iran war.

item.title: Hong Kong Deals Spring to Life With Over $4 Billion of Listings
item.description: Hong Kong is on track for at least 17 listings in June — the most since December — that are poised to raise as much as a combined $4.6 billion, with six of them taking investor orders from Wednesday.

item.title: South Africa Inflation Jumps Less Than Expected, Backs Rate Hold
item.description: South Africa’s annual inflation quickened less than expected in May and could ease in coming months after the US and Iran reached a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, providing room for the central bank to keep interest rates on hold.

item.title: Sweden Holds Rate at 1.75% and Signals Higher Chance of Hike
item.description: Sweden’s central bank held borrowing costs steady for a sixth straight meeting while indicating it’s ready to tighten policy this year if the Iran war triggers a rise in inflation.

item.title: Asia Reports Sharp Rise in Cybercrimes and Scams, Interpol Says
item.description: Illegal cyber activities accounted for around a third of all crimes recorded in some Asian countries, with scams the most widespread and financially damaging, according to a new Interpol report.

item.title: Protests at Rio Tinto’s Mongolia Copper Mine Disrupt Cargoes
item.description: A protest at Rio Tinto Group’s massive Oyu Tolgoi copper mine in Mongolia is threatening deliveries of concentrate, according to a social media post from the company’s local subsidiary.

item.title: SFC Moving Ahead With HK$1 Billion Evergrande Payout Plan
item.description: Hong Kong’s market regulator is proceeding with its plan to compensate minority shareholders of collapsed developer China Evergrande Group, brushing aside a legal challenge by the company’s liquidators.

item.title: Iran to Gain Major Financial Relief Under Interim Deal With US
item.description: Iran would be allowed to start oil exports immediately under an interim deal with the US and gain access to a $300 billion economic development program following negotiations for a permanent peace that’s meant to address Tehran’s nuclear activities, according a draft of their agreement.

item.title: Japan Tourism Extends Decline on Reduced Flights, China Weakness
item.description: Japan’s inbound tourism extended its decline in May, weighed down by reduced flights across routes and a prolonged slump in Chinese visitors.

item.title: DP World Seeks Return to US Container Operations at Texas Port
item.description: DP World is looking to operate a US container port for the first time in two decades.
